page: UpCloud PREMIUM-32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the architecture is x86_64. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a 500 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark data indicates strong compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top 10% tier. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and the PassMark CPU score place the instance in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks consistently rank in the top 25% tier, with read speeds reaching 5198.39 GB/sec for small blocks and 235.89 GB/sec for large blocks. However, the instance exhibits limitations in machine learning tasks, with LLM inference speeds for a small 135M model falling into the bottom 25% for prompt processing and the bottom 10% for text generation.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced hardware profile for general-purpose workloads. The shared CPU allocation model provides a cost-efficient alternative to dedicated-core instances for workloads that do not require continuous maximum CPU utilization, though it introduces potential performance variability under shared host load. Based on its strong database operations benchmark and average static web serving throughput, this server configuration is suited for relational databases, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and general-purpose application hosting, whereas it is poorly suited for LLM inference or GPU-accelerated workloads.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-32xCPU-128GB is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with a shared CPU allocation. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, a 500 GB local SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, placing it in the top 25% for PassMark CPU and database operations. However, it exhibits weak performance in LLM inference tasks. This resource profile is suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose application hosting where shared CPU allocation is acceptable.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-32xCPU-128GB is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 500 GB SSD storage, delivering strong database and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-32xCPU-128GB is an x86_64 virtual server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual server combining thirty-two AMD EPYC cores and high memory bandwidth for database and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 32 physical cores and x86_64 architecture
128 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 500 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth
Suited for database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and application hosting
Shared CPU allocation model offering balanced resource density
